concerns.text: The plan assumes Llama-3-8B can run DelTA (gradient backpropagation) on a GitHub Actions free-tier runner (2 CPU, 7GB RAM) within 6 hours. Llama-3-8B in full precision requires ~16GB+ RAM just to load weights, and gradient computation on CPU is orders of magnitude slower than GPU. This compute assumption is infeasible; the pipeline will OOM or timeout immediately. The plan must either switch to a quantized model (if allowed by constraints) or a significantly smaller model (e.g., Llama-3-1B or Phi-3-mini) to fit the 7GB RAM limit.

The plan defines 'Semantic Similarity' features using 'Llama-3-8B last-layer embedding space' for the seed set. While the plan forbids using the Oracle's hidden states for the *predictor* features, it relies on the same Llama-3-8B model to generate the static feature embeddings. If the Oracle step (Section 3.1) is infeasible due to RAM/CPU constraints, this feature extraction step is also infeasible. Furthermore, using the same model for feature generation as the Oracle risks subtle data leakage or circularity if the 'static' features implicitly encode the model's internal reasoning patterns, violating the 'Static-Input Independence' principle (Constitution Principle VI).
